rationale_for_baseline_tier: Dermatomal zoster needs prompt (same-day) antivirals within the therapeutic window and neuropathic analgesia; because a remote provider cannot ECG/auscultate, a cardiac/respiratory safety-net is retained. Escalate to T5 for cardiac/respiratory red flags or dissemination.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Crushing/exertional chest pain, radiation to arm/jaw with sweating, or true breathlessness/productive cough
escalation_edges.rationale: Would indicate a cardiac/respiratory emergency that cannot be excluded remotely.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the pain becomes crushing or spreads to your arm or jaw with sweating, or you become truly breathless or develop a productive cough — call 000 or go to the emergency department.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Rash spreading across the body/multiple areas, or involving the eye/forehead/nose tip
escalation_edges.rationale: Disseminated or ophthalmic zoster needs urgent specialist care.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the rash spreads across your whole body, or reaches your eye/forehead/nose tip, seek urgent medical care.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Higher postherpetic neuralgia risk — treat pain aggressively but titrate neuropathic agents cautiously; higher ACS pre-test probability keeps the cardiac safety-net important.
Partner can help monitor and arrange urgent transport if cardiac/respiratory features develop.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is shingles — start the antiviral now (it works best early) and the nerve-pain medicine for the burning. Keep the rash dry and uncovered and away from pregnant women and babies. Call 000 if the pain becomes crushing/spreads to the arm or jaw with sweating, or you become truly breathless.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Same-day GP for antivirals/analgesia; 000/ED for cardiac/respiratory red flags
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Use an after-hours GP to start antivirals promptly; ED/000 for cardiac or respiratory symptoms.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document thoracic herpes zoster within the antiviral window, cardiac/respiratory features screened, antivirals + neuropathic analgesia commenced, and PHN follow-up.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Attributing chest pain to zoster with no cardiac screen/safety-net, or not starting antivirals.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A concurrent cardiac event is missed, or the antiviral window is lost, worsening outcomes/postherpetic neuralgia.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine handling that delays antivirals beyond the window and under-treats the neuralgia.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Emergency ambulance for uncomplicated dermatomal zoster without cardiac/respiratory red flags.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot ECG/auscultate remotely to fully exclude a concurrent cardiac/respiratory cause of the chest pain.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat the confirmed dermatomal zoster promptly while screening for cardiac/respiratory features and retaining a clear emergency safety-net.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Attributing all chest pain to zoster with no cardiac safety-net, or delaying antivirals.
